hints.Beati and oculi: 'Beati' is an adjective and 'oculi' is a noun — 'qui' and 'quae' are relative pronouns
hints.oculi and qui: 'Oculi' is a noun meaning 'eyes' — both 'qui' and 'quae' are relative pronouns
hints.quae only: 'Quae' is one relative pronoun, but 'qui' is also a relative pronoun here

steps.reveal: qui = which (nom. masc. pl., referring to 'oculi')

hints.ablative: Ablative plural would be 'quibus' — 'qui' is nominative plural, subject of 'vident'
hints.dative: Dative plural would be 'quibus' — 'qui' is nominative
hints.genitive: Genitive plural would be 'quorum' — 'qui' is nominative

steps.reveal: quae = the things which (acc. neut. pl., object of 'videtis')

hints.nominative: While 'quae' can be nominative, here it is the object of 'videtis' — accusative neut. pl.
hints.ablative: Ablative feminine singular could be 'qua' — 'quae' here is accusative neut. pl.
hints.genitive: Genitive would be 'quarum/quorum' — 'quae' is accusative neut. pl.

hints.to come: That would be 'venire' — 'vident' comes from 'videre' (to see)
hints.to conquer: That would be 'vincere' — 'vident' comes from 'videre' (to see)
hints.to live: That would be 'vivere' — 'vident' comes from 'videre' (to see)

hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'videant' — 'vident' is present indicative
hints.perfect indicative: Perfect would be 'viderunt' — 'vident' is present indicative
hints.future indicative: Future would be 'videbunt' — 'vident' is present indicative

hints.vinco, vincere, vici, victum: These are the parts of 'vincere' (to conquer)
hints.vivo, vivere, vixi, victum: These are the parts of 'vivere' (to live)
hints.venio, venire, veni, ventum: These are the parts of 'venire' (to come)

hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'videatis' — 'videtis' is present indicative
hints.perfect indicative: Perfect would be 'vidistis' — 'videtis' is present indicative
hints.imperfect indicative: Imperfect would be 'videbatis' — 'videtis' is present indicative

hints.genitive: Genitive singular would be 'oculi', but here with 'Beati' (pl.) it is nominative plural
hints.dative: Dative would be 'oculis' — 'oculi' is nominative plural subject
hints.vocative: Vocative plural would also be 'oculi', but the eyes are being described, not addressed

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'oculi' ends in -i
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'oculi' ends in -i
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'oculi' ends in -i

hints.genitive: Genitive singular could be 'beati', but here it agrees with 'oculi' (nom. pl.)
hints.dative: Dative would be 'beatis' — 'Beati' is nominative, agreeing with 'oculi'
hints.vocative: Vocative plural would also be 'beati', but this is a declaration, not a direct address

translation: Blessed are the eyes which see the things that you see
